problem_summary: Replacing a cracked OLED display on an iPhone 12/13 with a like-for-like assembly.
safety_pre_check: Disconnect the battery flex before any other internal work.
Wear safety glasses — broken glass can flake off the display.
Use a magnetic mat to keep track of the tiny pentalobe screws.

parts_summary.name: Replacement OLED display assembly
parts_summary.specification: Genuine or aftermarket OEM-grade, matching the exact model number

scene_lock.subject: An iPhone 12/13 with a black aluminum frame and a cracked OLED display on the front.
scene_lock.environment: Clean lit workbench with a magnetic parts tray, anti-static mat, and bright overhead daylight LED panel.
scene_lock.hands_style: Adult hands wearing fitted blue nitrile gloves with anti-static fingertips.
scene_lock.style: Photorealistic instructional video style with crisp macro detail and clean even lighting.
scene_lock.color_palette: Charcoal phone, blue gloves, white workbench, silver tools, neutral overhead lighting.
consistency_phrases: Keep the iPhone identity (color, model, frame style) consistent across all shots
Show the cracked display only until the screen is replaced

steps.title: Power off and remove the bottom pentalobe screws
steps.description: Power the iPhone down completely, then unscrew the two pentalobe P2 screws on either side of the Lightning port at the bottom edge.
steps.subject_focus: The bottom edge of the iPhone and the two pentalobe screws.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Clean lit workbench. Subject: The iPhone lies face-up. A blue-gloved hand places a P2 pentalobe screwdriver into the small screw on the left side of the Lightning port. Details: Photorealistic macro, sharp screw threading visible, even overhead light.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same workbench. Subject: Both bottom pentalobe screws have been removed and placed on the magnetic tray next to the phone. Details: Photorealistic, screws clearly visible on the tray.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the gloved hand carefully unscrewing first the left, then the right pentalobe screw with controlled torque, lifting each free and placing it on the magnetic tray.
steps.narration: Power off the iPhone, then remove the two pentalobe screws on either side of the Lightning port.
steps.subtitle: Remove the bottom pentalobe screws.
steps.safety_note: Use the correct P2 bit — the wrong bit will strip the head.
steps.success_criteria: Both screws are out of the chassis and on the magnetic tray.
steps.common_mistake: Stripping the screw heads by using a Phillips bit.

steps.title: Lift the screen with a suction cup
steps.description: Stick a suction cup just above the home indicator and pull gently while sliding a plastic spudger around the perimeter to break the adhesive.
steps.subject_focus: The suction cup pulling the cracked screen up.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Same workbench. Subject: A black suction cup pressed firmly against the cracked OLED, just above the bottom edge. Details: Photorealistic macro, crack pattern still clearly visible.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same workbench. Subject: The screen is lifted a few millimeters off the chassis on the bottom edge while a plastic spudger slides along the side to break the adhesive seal. Details: Photorealistic, visible gap between screen and frame.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the suction cup being lifted gently while the spudger slides slowly along the perimeter, gradually opening the screen away from the chassis without flexing it.
steps.narration: Stick the suction cup near the bottom edge, lift gently, and run a plastic spudger around to break the adhesive.
steps.subtitle: Lift the screen with suction.
steps.safety_note: Open the phone like a book — never pull the screen fully off, the flex cables are still connected at the top.
steps.success_criteria: The screen lifts cleanly away from the chassis without cracking further.
steps.common_mistake: Pulling the screen straight up and tearing the display flex cables.

steps.title: Disconnect the flex cables and install the new display
steps.description: Remove the small Y000 bracket over the display connectors, gently lift the flex cables off the logic board, then reverse the process with the new screen.
steps.subject_focus: The display flex connectors and the Y000 bracket.
steps.visual_prompt_start: Scene: Same workbench. Subject: The phone is opened like a book, exposing the display flex connectors covered by a small metal bracket secured with tri-point Y000 screws. Details: Photorealistic macro, every screw and connector crisp.
steps.visual_prompt_end: Scene: Same workbench. Subject: The new replacement OLED display has been seated into the chassis, the flex connectors clicked back onto the logic board, and the bracket reinstalled. Details: Photorealistic, new screen perfectly aligned, no cracks visible.
steps.motion_prompt: Animate the Y000 screws being removed one by one, the bracket lifted away, the flex cables gently popped off the board with a spudger, then the new screen connected in reverse order.
steps.narration: Unscrew the bracket over the display connectors, pop the flex cables off the logic board, swap in the new screen, then reverse the process to reassemble.
steps.subtitle: Swap the display assembly.
steps.safety_note: Never reconnect a flex cable that isn't perfectly aligned — you'll short the board.
steps.success_criteria: The new screen powers on and shows a clean undamaged image.
steps.common_mistake: Forgetting to reseat the EMI bracket — the phone won't be water-resistant anymore.
